name: Ratner's Knishes
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first five ingredients and beat with an electric mixer until smooth. Pour a thin layer of oil over the dough. Let stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
2. Prepare the filling as directed.
3. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
4. On a heavily floured board, knead the dough until smooth and elastic. Roll out to a 1/8 inch thickness, shaping into an oblong measuring 14 x 24 inches. Brush the dough with oil.
5. Spoon the potato filling along one 24-inch edge of the dough. Spoon the vegetable filling along the other 24-inch edge.
6. Roll the dough over the filling and continue rolling towards the center. Then roll the other side towards the center in the same way. Separate the two rolls by cutting down the center. Cut each long roll into four pieces.
7. Place all four pieces on a heavily greased cookie sheet. Brush with oil and bake for 35-40 minutes, or until golden brown.
8. Remove from the oven and brush with egg wash. Return to the oven and bake an additional 10 minutes.
9. Cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces and serve warm.
NOTE: After cooling, knishes may be sliced, wrapped, and frozen (up to 1 year) and used when desired.
Yield: Four 12-inch lengths
VEGETABLE FILLING:
1. In a 10-inch skillet, heat the oil. Add onion, garlic, carrot, celery, mushrooms, and green pepper; sauté for 10 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
2. Stir in rice, kasha, and egg. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
3. Use as filling for knishes. Yield: 3 1/2 cups filling (enough for two 2-inch knishes)
POTATO FILLING:
1. In a skillet, heat butter and sauté onion until golden brown.
2. Stir in mashed potatoes, salt, and pepper to taste.
3. Cool.
4. Use as filling for knishes or rologines. Yield: Filling for 50 small rologines or two 12-inch knishes
